properties.name: Frankish Kingdom
properties.description: A simplified early Frankish realm in northern Gaul and the Rhineland. Clovis I expanded Frankish power and his conversion to Catholic Christianity helped shape medieval Western Europe.

properties.name: Visigothic Kingdom
properties.description: A major post-Roman kingdom ruling much of Iberia and parts of southern Gaul. Around 500 CE its center was still connected to Toulouse before later shifting toward Toledo.

properties.name: Ostrogothic Kingdom
properties.description: The Ostrogothic Kingdom controlled Italy after Theoderic defeated Odoacer. It preserved many Roman administrative traditions while ruling as a Gothic kingdom.

properties.name: Byzantine Empire
properties.description: The Eastern Roman Empire remained the dominant imperial power in the eastern Mediterranean. Around 500 CE it controlled Constantinople, the Balkans, Greece, Anatolia, and parts of the eastern Mediterranean.

properties.name: Anglo-Saxon Kingdoms
properties.description: A simplified representation of early Anglo-Saxon polities in Britain. Around 500 CE the region was politically fragmented and still undergoing major cultural and religious change.
